meta.title: QA Testing Report
meta.project: Inaza Voice Bot — FNOL Afterhours Agent
meta.confidentiality: Internal use only — do not distribute

summary.overview: SNAP Insurance conducted a controlled quality assurance test of Inaza's afterhours FNOL voice agent using 10 internal testers across 14 real claim scenarios. Testers were assigned characters, moods, and complete claim data to simulate authentic customer interactions. Results were evaluated across two dimensions: user experience (tester survey) and data accuracy (Modotech system comparison).

testers.name: Edmar
testers.scenario: Parking lot backup — Grand Prairie, TX
testers.bestPart: How she acknowledges the information I spelled out.
testers.worstPart: The end of the call — she provides the closing way too fast and it is confusing. When I asked her to repeat, she sounded a bit rude.
testers.otherNotes: During the call Lucy sounded professional but distant and sometimes rude. If I had to ask her to repeat, she sounded irritated.
dataIssues: Accident location logged as Unknown
Insured name truncated to 'One Hernandez' — first name dropped
No address fields collected for any party
CV make, model, color not captured
CV damage not captured
Police report not logged
Call length over 10 minutes for a simple parking lot claim
testers.callbackGravity: Adjuster must re-ask accident location, full driver name, all address fields, and complete CV details — customer repeats themselves on core claim facts.

testers.name: Mary Alejos
testers.worstPart: The call was dropped when I was going to be connected with an agent.
testers.otherNotes: The bot read the VIN back correctly but wasn't able to verify the vehicle on the first try.
dataIssues: Zero data saved — no claim created on either attempt
Lucy got stuck on passenger phone number — non-essential field blocked flow
Transfer to live agent triggered — call dropped both times
No fallback — all collected data lost on transfer failure
testers.callbackGravity: No claim exists. Customer must start completely from scratch. Total data loss on both attempts.

testers.name: JC
testers.scenario: First accident ever — hit parked car without realizing, Ohio
testers.bestPart: She took the details well, waited while I obtained the VIN, and repeated things slower so I could write them down.
testers.worstPart: On my first attempt I said I was the policyholder and she confused this as the claimant and tried to transfer me to a live agent.
testers.otherNotes: She was warm and slightly comforting. When I asked if I was in trouble she said she understood I was worried but needed the facts.
dataIssues: Role confusion: 'policyholder' misidentified as claimant on attempt 1 — triggered transfer
VIN transcription error: Z vs V (1GCRCREC9FZ vs 1GCRCREC9FV)
CV year logged as '0'
CV damage shows 'no damage' despite significant front-end damage in scenario
CV injured status conflict — scenario said No, system logged Yes
CV tow status conflict — scenario said not towed, system logged tow date
Police report number not captured
testers.callbackGravity: Adjuster must verify VIN, CV damage, injury status, and tow status. Role confusion caused a failed first attempt — customer had to call twice.

testers.name: Claudia
testers.scenario: Rear-ended a RAV4 on Texas highway — Dallas, TX
testers.role: Insured — cooperative but in a hurry
testers.bestPart: Lucy was very kind, understanding, moved fast, was clear. She almost felt like I was talking to a live person.
testers.worstPart: Just trying to trust a robot to take down my info. As a customer I'm wondering if this is a scam or if I legit just filed a claim.
testers.otherNotes: Who can I call to confirm it was filed? How long am I waiting? Would I have the option of speaking to someone live?
dataIssues: CV year logged as '0'
CV damage shows 'no damage' despite rear bumper damage in scenario
CV VIN not captured
Driver name misspelled: Parra → 'Para'
No closing summary — customer left with no confirmation, no next steps, no timeline
testers.callbackGravity: Claim filed accurately on IV side. Customer left confused about whether claim was real. No confirmation communicated — trust gap despite perfect call ratings.

testers.name: Mary Uribe
testers.scenario: Driving sister-in-law's car, rear-ended a Range Rover — McAllen, TX
testers.worstPart: Have Lucy speak a little slower.
dataIssues: Policyholder vs driver distinction lost — driver logged as vehicle owner
Incident description not captured — replaced by caller's repeated question about the car
CV year logged as '0'
Time of loss not captured
Police report not logged
Tow information not captured
Driver name misspelled: Marycita → 'Maristita'
testers.callbackGravity: Adjuster must reconstruct the incident description entirely. Owner/driver relationship incorrectly established — may affect coverage determination.

testers.name: Alex
testers.scenario: Rear-ended in stop-and-go traffic — Military Pkwy, Dallas TX
testers.whatWasMissed: No claimant personal information collected — name, phone, address, injury status
testers.bestPart: Lucy seemed to understand everything I was saying and allowed me to interrupt to correct the VIN.
testers.worstPart: Lucy failed to ask any information from me — my name, if I was injured, or any damages to my vehicle.
dataIssues: CV entirely wrong — Lucy used IV make/model for CV (Mitsubishi instead of Camaro)
CV plate wrong — IV plate used for CV
CV owner wrong — 'Luiz Gizanda' instead of Florence Kempf
CV insurance set to SNAP instead of Texas Farm Bureau
CV damage copied from IV description
Claimant personal info never collected — name, phone, address, injury status all missing
VIN collected twice
IV address incorrect — different address in same city
testers.callbackGravity: CV is entirely mirrored from IV data. Claimant — the person who called — has no record in the system. Adjuster must re-interview both parties. Claim cannot be properly worked as filed.

testers.name: Ron
testers.scenario: Calling for wife — SNAP insured ran red light, Las Vegas NM
testers.worstPart: She spoke a little too fast.
dataIssues: CV year logged as '0'
CV damage not captured
IV owner confused with driver — Tiffany logged as owner, policyholder is Anthony Mark Sanchez
Claimant name misspelled: Amirra → 'Amiri'
Witness not captured
Lucy created claim on policy with active 'do not change' flag — existing open claim 2026-WINM03317
Speech rate noted as too fast
testers.callbackGravity: Duplicate claim created on flagged policy. Adjuster must reconcile with existing claim 2026-WINM03317 and verify CV damage and witness information.

testers.name: Sarai
testers.scenario: Sideswiped merging on HWY 83 — McAllen, TX
testers.bestPart: She handled the conversation better than any other version. Best version of Lucy I've experienced.
testers.worstPart: Lucy's non-human moments — interrupting mid-sentence, not giving a full moment. VIN pronunciation threw me off.
testers.otherNotes: When I gave the wrong claimant phone number and told Lucy, she said we'd come back to it. We never did. Also — should we be confirming DOB and DL for the calling driver? My driver was excluded.
dataIssues: Policy had NO COVERAGE on date of loss — Lucy unaware of coverage gaps
Wrong policy matched — VIN matched but policy period not active on March 17
Claimant phone number error unresolved — Lucy deferred correction, never returned
CV make/model not captured
CV year not captured
CV damage not captured
CV driver name misspelled: Sheila → 'Shayla'
IV color misspelled: Grey → 'Grat'
Lucy interrupts caller mid-sentence
VIN read back too fast to verify
testers.callbackGravity: Coverage gap not detected — claim filed on inactive policy. Wrong phone number in system unresolved. CV entirely missing. Adjuster must verify coverage validity before proceeding.

testers.name: JO
testers.scenario: Policyholder not present — uncle driving, Mesquite TX
testers.bestPart: Lucy understood and repeated the information after it was given which made me aware she heard it correctly.
testers.worstPart: I honestly did not find anything frustrating or confusing.
dataIssues: Zero data saved — no claim created
Call completed perfectly — highest bot rating of the round (10/10)
Lucy escalated to live agent at end of call
Transfer dropped — all data lost
Policy was cancelled on 06/28/2026 — Lucy unaware of policy status
testers.callbackGravity: Perfect call experience, zero system output. Customer left confident a claim was filed. No claim exists. Total data loss on the highest-rated call of the round.

testers.name: Judy
testers.scenario: Hit fixed object — RV park gate, Harlingen TX
testers.whatWasRepeated: CV information asked on a property-damage-only claim
testers.bestPart: Clear questions. Overall not bad — liked her voice better than others I've heard.
testers.worstPart: She said the VIN back way too fast.
testers.otherNotes: What was that random 'hmmm' or whatever that was in the background? Questions were asked a little too quickly.
dataIssues: Police report not captured despite being provided
Driver injured status conflict — scenario said unknown, system logged Yes
CV information asked on PD-only claim — no CV vehicle exists
VIN read back too fast to verify
Background noise artifact noted — audible 'hmmm' during call
testers.callbackGravity: Minor issues only — police report and injury status can be verified in a single callback without requiring customer to re-explain the incident. Best data quality result of the round.

patterns.issue: CV year never captured — logs as '0'
Transfer to live agent = call drops, total data loss
No closing summary — customer doesn't know what happens next
Lucy unaware of policy status (cancelled, flagged, coverage gaps)
CV injured status conflicts with reported scenario
Role identification causes confusion or failed attempt
Tone perceived as rude or irritated when asked to repeat
Police report number not logged despite being provided
VIN read back too fast to verify
Background noise artifact in Lucy's audio

recommendations.issue: Transfer to live agent breaks the call — no fallback saves the data
recommendations.detail: 3 out of 10 calls resulted in zero data when Lucy transferred to a live agent. The transfer fails and everything is lost. A fallback must save the claim before any transfer is attempted.

recommendations.issue: CV year field never populated — consistently logs as '0'
recommendations.detail: Every single case with a claimant vehicle shows year '0' in Modotech. This is a systemic mapping failure between Lucy's data collection and the system field.

recommendations.issue: Closing script missing — customers don't know if claim was filed
recommendations.detail: Testers with 10/10 ratings still left confused about whether a claim was actually filed. Lucy must end every call with: claim number, what happens next, expected callback timeframe, and a number to call for confirmation.

recommendations.issue: When caller is a claimant, Lucy collects IV data only — CV data is missing or mirrored from IV
recommendations.detail: Case 12 showed CV entirely built from IV data. Lucy's flow appears optimized for insured callers, not claimants. When the caller is the claimant, all their personal information must be collected as the primary party.

recommendations.issue: Lucy has no awareness of policy status — cancelled, flagged, or coverage gaps
recommendations.detail: Lucy created claims on a cancelled policy, a flagged policy with an existing open claim, and a policy with no coverage on the date of loss. A policy status check must be integrated into the opening of every call.

recommendations.issue: CV damage defaults to 'no damage' — Lucy not confirming claimant vehicle damage
recommendations.detail: 4 of 10 cases show CV damage as 'no damage' despite scenarios with clear damage. Lucy must explicitly ask for and confirm CV damage before closing.

recommendations.issue: Speech rate too fast — testers consistently struggled
recommendations.detail: 4 of 10 testers independently noted Lucy speaks too fast. This is especially problematic during VIN readback and closing summary. A slower default rate is recommended, especially for confirmation sequences.
4 of 10 cases had driver names misspelled due to phonetic transcription (Marycita → Maristita, Amirra → Amiri, Sheila → Shayla, Parra → Para). A confirmation step for names is recommended.

recommendations.issue: Tone on repetition — perceived as rude or irritated
recommendations.detail: 2 testers noted Lucy sounded rude or irritated when asked to repeat information. This is a tone calibration issue in the repetition response script.

recommendations.issue: Deferred corrections not followed up — 'we'll come back to it' never happens
recommendations.detail: When a caller provided a correction, Lucy deferred it and never returned. Any deferred item must be queued and confirmed before the call ends.
